    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\old\\US4TC\\US4GROUP\\APP\\controllers\\admin\\Extension.php",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 156
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 196
          }
        },
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "        $total_list = $this-&gt;total_list($table_name,$admin_id );",
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "        $output = array(",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"draw\" =&gt; $draw,",
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"recordsTotal\" =&gt; $total_list,",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"recordsFiltered\" =&gt; $total_list,",
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "            \"data\" =&gt; $data",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "text": "        );",
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "        echo json_encode($output);",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "text": "        exit();",
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "text": "\t",
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "text": "\tpublic function total_list($table_name,$user_id)",
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "text": "    {",
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$this-&gt;db-&gt;where('SipType',1);",
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "        $this-&gt;db-&gt;where('AdminID',$user_id);",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "        $query = $this-&gt;db-&gt;select(\"COUNT(*) as num\")-&gt;get($table_name);",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "        $result = $query-&gt;row();",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "        if(isset($result)) return $result-&gt;num;",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "        return 0;",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "\tpublic function addExtension()",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "\t{",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$controller_view_path=APPPATH.'views/admin/Extension/addExtension.php';",
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$controller_view=\"admin/Extension/addExtension\";",
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "text": "        if ( ! file_exists($controller_view_path))",
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "        {",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "text": "                // Whoops, we don't have a page for that!",
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "text": "                show_404();",
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$this-&gt;check_session(\"admin\");",
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$user_id=$this-&gt;session-&gt;userdata('user_id');",
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$this-&gt;session-&gt;set_flashdata('success', 'You are successfuly logged in.');",
            "lineNumber": 188
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$data['title'] = ucfirst('ADD Extension');",
            "lineNumber": 189
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$data['session'] = $this-&gt;session-&gt;userdata();",
            "lineNumber": 190
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$this-&gt;load_views_site($data, [$controller_view]);",
            "lineNumber": 191
          },
          {
            "text": "\t}",
            "lineNumber": 192
          },
          {
            "text": "\t",
            "lineNumber": 193
          },
          {
            "text": "\tpublic function editExtension($args)",
            "lineNumber": 194
          },
          {
            "text": "\t{",
            "lineNumber": 195
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$ExtensionID=$args[0];",
            "lineNumber": 196
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.3193542957305908
    },

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 260
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t\t\"dtmfmode\"\t\t\t\t\t\t=&gt;\t\t$dtmf_mode,",

          {
            "text": "\t\t\t\t\"host\"\t\t\t\t\t\t\t=&gt;\t\t$host_ip,",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 263
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t\t\"MyCallerID\"\t\t\t\t\t=&gt;\t\t$caller_id,",

          {

            "lineNumber": 266
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t",
            "lineNumber": 267
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t$asterisk_sip_extension_file = $this-&gt;config-&gt;item(\"asterisk_sip_extension_file\");",
            "lineNumber": 268
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\tif(file_exists($asterisk_sip_extension_file))",
            "lineNumber": 269
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t{",
            "lineNumber": 270
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t$asterisk_sip_extension_file_data = file_get_contents($asterisk_sip_extension_file);",
            "lineNumber": 271
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t$asterisk_sip_extension_detail_sample = $this-&gt;config-&gt;item(\"asterisk_sip_extension_detail_sample\");",
            "lineNumber": 272
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t$extension_detail = \"disallow=all\\nallow=ulaw\\nallow=alaw\\ncontext=$context\\nsecret=$extension_secret\\ndtmfmode=$dtmf_mode\\nhost=$host_ip\\ntrustrpid=$trustrpid\\nsendrpid=$senderpid\\ntype=$type\\nnat=$nat\\nqualify=$qualify\\nqualifyfreq=60\\ntransport=$transport\\nmailbox=$past_extension_name@device\\ncallerid=$past_extension_name\\ncallcounter=yes\";",
            "lineNumber": 273
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t$past_extension_detail = str_replace(array(\"&lt;AST-SIP-EXTENSION-NAME&gt;\", \"&lt;AST-SIP-EXTENSION-DETAIL&gt;\"), array($past_extension_name, $past_extension_detail),$asterisk_sip_extension_detail_sample);",
            "lineNumber": 274
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t$sip_extension_detail = str_replace(array(\"&lt;AST-SIP-EXTENSION-NAME&gt;\", \"&lt;AST-SIP-EXTENSION-DETAIL&gt;\"), array($past_extension_name, $extension_detail),$asterisk_sip_extension_detail_sample);",
            "lineNumber": 275
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t$asterisk_sip_extension_file_updated_data=str_replace($past_extension_detail, $sip_extension_detail,$asterisk_sip_extension_file_data,$ReplacementCount);",
            "lineNumber": 276
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t",
            "lineNumber": 277
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\tif($ReplacementCount&gt;=1)",

          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t{",
            "lineNumber": 279
          },
          {
            "text": "\t\t\t\tfile_put_contents($asterisk_sip_extension_file, $asterisk_sip_extension_file_updated_data);",

          }

            "text": ";",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "text": "; Possible driver backends:",

          },
          {
            "text": ";",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "; \"odbc\" is shown in the examples below, but is not the only valid realtime",

            "text": "; engine.  Here are several of the possible options:",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },

            "text": ";    odbc ... res_config_odbc",

            "text": ";    sqlite ... res_config_sqlite",

            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": ";    ldap ... res_config_ldap",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": ";    mysql ... res_config_mysql (available via add-ons in menuselect)",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": ";",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "; Note: The res_config_pgsql and res_config_sqlite backends configure the",

          {
            "text": "; While most dynamic realtime engines are automatically used when defined in",

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I found the main pieces:

1. **SuperAdmin page** is at `src/pages/SuperAdmin.jsx` - it's a React component with a simple dashboard (stat cards and quick action buttons). No backend API for superadmin user/SIP management in the new app yet.

2. **Database schema** (`docs/database-schema.sql`) has:

Here’s a concise implementation guide for what you described. I’m in **Ask mode**, so this is guidance only; I can’t edit the repo. If you want the changes applied in the project, switch to **Agent mode**.

---

## Current state

- **Super Admin page:** `src/pages/SuperAdmin.jsx` — stat cards + quick actions (Manage tenants, System settings, etc.). No “Add user/agent/admin/superadmin” or “Users list” / “SIP extensions table” yet.
- **Backend:** Only `/api/auth` and `/api/agent`. No superadmin-only APIs for users or SIP extensions. User creation is CLI-only via `server/add-user.js`.
- **Data:** `users` (role: 1=superadmin, 2=admin, 3=user, 5=agent; agents use `parent_id` as tenant), and `sip_extensions` (tenant_id, name, secret, context, host, …). There is no `tenants` table; `tenant_id` is a number (e.g. seed uses `tenant_id = 2`).

---

## 1. Super Admin dashboard – buttons and sections

In **Super Admin**, add:

- **Buttons:** “Add user”, “Add agent”, “Add admin”, “Add superadmin” (each can open the same “Create user” form with role preselected or a role dropdown).
- **Sections (or sub-views):**
  - **Users list** – table of all users (id, username, email, role, parent_id, account_status, created_at). Optional: filter by role, edit/disable/delete.
  - **SIP extensions** – table of all SIP extensions (id, tenant_id, name, secret, context, host, type, created_at). Optional: filter by tenant_id, add/edit/delete.

So the superadmin can create users and SIP extensions via the GUI and see the full users list and SIP extension table.

---

## 2. Backend – superadmin-only API

Add a **superadmin** route module (e.g. `server/routes/superadmin.js`) and mount it only for authenticated superadmins (e.g. middleware that checks `req.session.user.role === 'superadmin'`).

**Users:**

- **GET `/api/superadmin/users`**  
  - Query: `users` (join role names if you keep a role lookup).  
  - Return: `{ success: true, users: [...] }` with id, username, email, role (id and/or name), parent_id, account_status, created_at.

- **POST `/api/superadmin/users`**  
  - Body: `username`, `password`, `role` (superadmin | admin | user | agent), optional `email`, optional `parent_id` (required for agents – tenant).  
  - Hash password (e.g. same as `add-user.js` with bcrypt), insert into `users`.  
  - Return: `{ success: true, user: { id, username, role, ... } }`.

**SIP extensions:**

- **GET `/api/superadmin/sip-extensions`**  
  - Query: `sip_extensions` (optionally by tenant_id).  
  - Return: `{ success: true, extensions: [...] }` (id, tenant_id, name, secret, context, host, type, created_at, etc.).

- **POST `/api/superadmin/sip-extensions`**  
  - Body: `tenant_id`, `name`, optional `secret`, `context`, `host`, `type`, etc., matching your schema.  
  - Insert into `sip_extensions`.  
  - Return: `{ success: true, extension: { id, tenant_id, name, ... } }`.

Optional: PUT/DELETE for users and extensions if you want edit/delete from the GUI.  
Use the same DB helpers (`query` / `queryOne`) and role constants (e.g. from `server/auth.js`) so behaviour matches `add-user.js` and the schema.

---

## 3. Frontend – flows and UI

- **Super Admin page**
  - Add the four buttons (Add user / agent / admin / superadmin). They can either:
    - Navigate to a “Create user” page with a query/state for role, or  
    - Open a modal with a form: username, password, role (dropdown), email, and for role=agent a “Tenant (parent_id)” field.
  - Add two clear entry points: “View users list” and “View SIP extensions” (buttons or tabs that show the tables below or on separate routes).

- **Users list**
  - Fetch `GET /api/superadmin/users` and render a table (username, email, role, parent_id, status, created_at). Optional: search/filter by role, “Add user” opening the same form.

- **SIP extensions table**
  - Fetch `GET /api/superadmin/sip-extensions` and render a table (tenant_id, name, secret, context, host, type, created_at). Optional: filter by tenant_id.

- **Create user form**
  - On submit: `POST /api/superadmin/users` with the chosen role (and parent_id for agents). Then refresh the users list or redirect back to it.

- **Create SIP extension form**
  - Form: tenant_id, name, secret, context, host, type, etc. Submit: `POST /api/superadmin/sip-extensions`. Then refresh the extensions table.

Use your existing `Layout` and `Dashboard.css` so the new sections match the current dashboard style; reuse the same `action-btn` / `card-grid` patterns where it fits.

---

## 4. Important details

- **Auth:** All superadmin endpoints must require login and `role === 'superadmin'` (middleware that checks `req.session.user` from the same session used by `/api/auth`).
- **Tenant / parent_id:** Schema has no `tenants` table. For “create users and SIP extensions”:
  - **Users:** For role `agent`, set `parent_id` to the tenant id they belong to (e.g. 2). Other roles can have `parent_id` null unless you use it for hierarchy.
  - **SIP extensions:** Always set `tenant_id` (e.g. same numeric id as used for agents’ `parent_id`). In the GUI you can use a number input or, later, a dropdown when you add a tenants table.
- **CORS / cookie:** Keep `credentials: true` and same-origin (or correct CORS) so the session cookie is sent with these API calls from the React app.

---

## 5. File-level checklist (for when you or Agent implement it)

| Area | File(s) | Change |
|------|--------|--------|
| API | `server/routes/superadmin.js` | New: middleware + GET/POST users, GET/POST sip-extensions |
| API | `server/index.js` | Mount `superadmin` routes under `/api/superadmin` with superadmin-only middleware |
| Frontend | `src/pages/SuperAdmin.jsx` | Add buttons (Add user/agent/admin/superadmin, View users, View SIP extensions) and state/routing for list + create views |
| Frontend | Optional: `src/pages/SuperAdminUsers.jsx` and `SuperAdminSipExtensions.jsx` (or inline sections) | Users table + create user form; SIP extensions table + create extension form |
| Frontend | `src/App.jsx` | If you use separate pages, add routes under `/superadmin` (e.g. `/superadmin/users`, `/superadmin/sip-extensions`) |

If you want this implemented in the repo step by step, switch to **Agent mode** and ask to add the superadmin users + SIP extensions GUI and API as above.

Summary of what’s in place:

### Backend

- **`server/routes/superadmin.js`**
  - `requireSuperadmin` middleware: only logged-in users with role `superadmin` can call these routes.
  - **GET `/api/superadmin/users`** – returns all users (id, username, email, role, parent_id, account_status, created_at).
  - **POST `/api/superadmin/users`** – creates a user (body: `username`, `password`, `role` = superadmin|admin|user|agent, optional `email`, optional `parent_id` for agents).
  - **GET `/api/superadmin/sip-extensions`** – returns all SIP extensions (optional query `tenant_id`).
  - **POST `/api/superadmin/sip-extensions`** – creates an extension (body: `tenant_id`, `name`, optional `secret`, `context`, `host`, `type`).

- **`server/index.js`** – superadmin routes mounted at `/api/superadmin`.

### Frontend (Super Admin dashboard)

- **Quick actions**
  - **Add superadmin / Add admin / Add user / Add agent** – open the “Create user” form with the chosen role and switch to the Users view.
  - **View users list** – shows the users table.
  - **View SIP extensions** – shows the SIP extensions table.
  - **Back to overview** – returns to the system overview.
  - Existing actions (Manage tenants, System settings, Audit logs, Backup & restore) are still there.

- **Users view**
  - Table: ID, Username, Email, Role, Parent ID, Status, Created.
  - “Add user” button opens the create form (username, password, role, email, tenant/parent_id for agents). Submit calls the API and refreshes the list.

- **SIP extensions view**
  - Table: ID, Tenant ID, Name, Secret (masked), Context, Host, Type, Created.
  - “Add SIP extension” button opens the create form (tenant_id, name, secret, context, host, type). Submit calls the API and refreshes the list.

- **`src/pages/SuperAdmin.css`** – styles for the tables and forms.

**How to test**

1. Use **real API auth** (not demo): run `npm run server` and ensure the app uses the API (e.g. `VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3001` if needed).
2. Log in as **superadmin** with the seeded user (e.g. username `superadmin`, password `demo123`).
3. Open the Super Admin dashboard and use “Add user” / “Add agent” (etc.), “View users list”, and “View SIP extensions” to create and list users and SIP extensions.

Demo-only login (no API) does not send a session cookie, so superadmin API calls will return 401 until you use the real API login.
